---
title: "Plot"
---

The `plot` module turns analysis outputs into manuscript-ready tables and
figures. It is intentionally narrow: forest plots for effect estimates and
Table 1 outputs for baseline characteristics.

## Scope

| Function | Role |
|---|---|
| `plot_forest()` | Render effect estimates with confidence intervals |
| `plot_tableone()` | Build baseline characteristic tables with optional p-values and SMDs |

## Workflow Role

`plot_forest()` is designed for outputs from `assoc_*()` and `grs_validate()`.
`plot_tableone()` is designed for cohort description before modelling. Both
functions keep common publication defaults close to the workflow while still
allowing manual formatting for specific manuscripts.

## Review Focus

- stable column indexing after forest-plot display columns are inserted;
- numeric validation for estimates, confidence intervals, and p-value columns;
- clear save behavior and supported output formats;
- optional dependencies for document and image export.
